sentences.sentence: Look around on your next plane trip.
sentences.translation: 下次坐飞机时，看看你周围的情景。

structure.text: Look around
on your next plane trip

difficult_points: 这是祈使句，用来引导读者观察一个日常场景。
on your next plane trip 表示观察发生的具体情境，不是动作方向。

sentences.sentence: The iPad is the new pacifier for babies and toddlers.
sentences.translation: iPad 成了婴儿和幼儿的新安抚奶嘴。

structure.text: The iPad
is the new pacifier for babies and toddlers

difficult_points: pacifier 本义是安抚奶嘴，这里比喻电子设备被用来安抚幼儿。
new 暗示现代家庭中平板替代了传统安抚方式。

sentences.sentence: Younger school-aged children read stories on smartphones; older kids don't read at all, but hunch over video games.
sentences.translation: 较小的学龄儿童在智能手机上读故事；较大的孩子根本不读书，而是弓着身子玩电子游戏。

structure.text: Younger school-aged children
read stories on smartphones
older kids don't read at all
but hunch over video games

difficult_points: 分号连接两个对照场景：较小孩子用手机读，较大孩子不读而玩游戏。
don't read at all 强调完全不读，语气很强。
hunch over 描写身体姿态，暗示沉浸在屏幕前。

sentences.sentence: Parents and other passengers read on tablets or skim a flotilla of email and news feeds.
sentences.translation: 父母和其他乘客在平板上阅读，或者快速浏览大量邮件和新闻推送。

structure.text: Parents and other passengers
read on tablets
or skim a flotilla of email and news feeds

difficult_points: or 连接两个并列阅读行为：read 和 skim。
skim 表示快速浏览，与深度阅读形成隐含对比。
a flotilla of 本义为小船队，这里形象地表示大量信息流。

sentences.sentence: Unbeknown to most of us, an invisible, game-changing transformation links everyone in this picture: the neuronal circuit that underlies the brain's ability to read is subtly, rapidly changing and this has implications for everyone from the pre-reading toddler to the expert adult.
sentences.translation: 我们大多数人并未察觉，一场看不见却会改变格局的转变把画面中的每个人联系在一起：支撑大脑阅读能力的神经回路正在微妙而迅速地变化，而这对从尚未学会阅读的幼儿到阅读娴熟的成年人都有影响。

structure.text: Unbeknown to most of us
an invisible, game-changing transformation
links everyone in this picture
the neuronal circuit that underlies the brain's ability to read is subtly, rapidly changing
that underlies the brain's ability to read
and this has implications for everyone from the pre-reading toddler to the expert adult

difficult_points: Unbeknown to most of us 是句首补充成分，表示这种变化多数人没有意识到。
冒号后解释 invisible transformation 的具体内容。
that underlies... 修饰 neuronal circuit，说明该回路支撑阅读能力。
from...to... 表示影响范围覆盖不同阅读阶段的人。

sentences.sentence: As work in neurosciences indicates, the acquisition of literacy necessitated a new circuit in our species' brain more than 6,000 years ago.
sentences.translation: 正如神经科学研究所显示的那样，六千多年前，识字能力的获得使人类大脑中必须形成一条新的回路。

structure.text: As work in neurosciences indicates
the acquisition of literacy
necessitated a new circuit in our species' brain
more than 6,000 years ago

difficult_points: As 引导方式或依据状语从句，表示后文说法来自神经科学研究。
the acquisition of literacy 是名词化主语，核心是 acquisition。
necessitated 表示“使……成为必要”，强调阅读回路不是天生现成的。

sentences.sentence: That circuit evolved from a very simple mechanism for decoding basic information, like the number of goats in one's herd, to the present, highly elaborated reading brain.
sentences.translation: 那条回路从一种用于解码基本信息的非常简单的机制演化而来，例如识别自己畜群中山羊的数量，最终发展为如今高度复杂的阅读大脑。

structure.text: That circuit
evolved from a very simple mechanism for decoding basic information
like the number of goats in one's herd
to the present, highly elaborated reading brain

difficult_points: from...to... 构成演化路径，从简单机制到复杂阅读大脑。
like the number of goats in one's herd 是例子，说明 basic information 的类型。
highly elaborated 表示结构和功能高度发展，不是“精心装饰”。

sentences.sentence: My research depicts how the present reading brain enables the development of some of our most important intellectual and affective processes: internalized knowledge, analogical reasoning, and inference; perspective-taking and empathy; critical analysis and the generation of insight.
sentences.translation: 我的研究描绘了当今阅读大脑如何促成我们一些最重要的智力和情感过程的发展：内化知识、类比推理与推断；换位思考和共情；批判性分析以及洞见的生成。

structure.text: My research
depicts
how the present reading brain enables the development of some of our most important intellectual and affective processes
internalized knowledge, analogical reasoning, and inference; perspective-taking and empathy; critical analysis and the generation of insight

difficult_points: how 引导 depicts 的宾语从句，说明研究描绘的内容。
enables the development of 表示阅读大脑促成多种高级过程的发展。
冒号后的并列名词组具体列举 intellectual and affective processes。
分号用于区分三组复杂并列项目。

sentences.sentence: Research surfacing in many parts of the world now cautions that each of these essential 'deep reading' processes may be under threat as we move into digital-based modes of reading.
sentences.translation: 如今，世界许多地区不断出现的研究警示说，随着我们转向基于数字媒介的阅读模式，这些必要的“深度阅读”过程中的每一个都可能受到威胁。

structure.text: Research surfacing in many parts of the world
surfacing in many parts of the world
now cautions
that each of these essential 'deep reading' processes may be under threat
as we move into digital-based modes of reading

difficult_points: surfacing in many parts of the world 是现在分词短语，修饰 Research。
cautions that... 表示研究提出警示，而不只是中性说明。
as 引导时间或背景状语从句，说明威胁出现的时代背景。
each of these... 强调每一种深度阅读过程都可能受影响。

expressions.meaning_zh: 基于数字媒介的阅读模式

sentences.sentence: This is not a simple, binary issue of print versus digital reading and technological innovation.
sentences.translation: 这并不是一个关于纸质阅读与数字阅读、以及技术创新之间简单二元对立的问题。

structure.text: This
is not a simple, binary issue of print versus digital reading and technological innovation

difficult_points: This 指代前文关于数字阅读威胁深度阅读的讨论。
not a simple, binary issue 否定把问题简化成二选一。
print versus digital reading 表示纸质和数字阅读之间的对照。

expressions.meaning_zh: 纸质阅读与数字阅读之争

sentences.sentence: As MIT scholar Sherry Turkle has written, we do not err as a society when we innovate but when we ignore what we disrupt or diminish while innovating.
sentences.translation: 正如麻省理工学院学者雪莉·特克尔所写，作为一个社会，我们犯错并不是在创新时，而是在创新过程中忽视了自己扰乱或削弱了什么时。

structure.text: As MIT scholar Sherry Turkle has written
we
do not err as a society
when we innovate
but when we ignore what we disrupt or diminish while innovating
what we disrupt or diminish while innovating

difficult_points: As 引导引用来源，说明后面观点来自 Sherry Turkle。
not...but... 构成对比，强调错误不在创新本身，而在忽视创新造成的损失。
what we disrupt or diminish 是名词性从句，作 ignore 的宾语。
while innovating 表示扰乱或削弱发生在创新过程中。

sentences.sentence: In this hinge moment between print and digital cultures, society needs to confront what is diminishing in the expert reading circuit, what our children and older students are not developing, and what we can do about it.
sentences.translation: 在纸质文化与数字文化之间的这个关键转折时刻，社会需要直面专家阅读回路中正在减弱的东西、我们的孩子和高年级学生没有发展起来的能力，以及我们能对此做些什么。

structure.text: In this hinge moment between print and digital cultures
society
needs to confront
what is diminishing in the expert reading circuit
what our children and older students are not developing
and what we can do about it

difficult_points: hinge moment 比喻关键转折点，指两种阅读文化交替的时期。
needs to confront 后接三个并列 what 从句，都是需要面对的问题。
expert reading circuit 指成熟读者的大脑阅读回路。
it 指代前面这些阅读能力受损的问题。

sentences.sentence: We know from research that the reading circuit is not given to human beings through a genetic blueprint like vision or language; it needs an environment to develop.
sentences.translation: 我们从研究中知道，阅读回路并不像视觉或语言那样通过基因蓝图赋予人类；它需要环境才能发展。

structure.text: We
know from research
that the reading circuit is not given to human beings through a genetic blueprint like vision or language
it needs an environment to develop

difficult_points: that 引导 know 的宾语从句，说明研究结论。
is not given...through a genetic blueprint 是被动结构，表示阅读回路并非由基因直接赋予。
分号连接两个密切相关的分句，第二句解释阅读回路需要后天环境。
like vision or language 是对 genetic blueprint 的类比对象。

sentences.sentence: Further, it will adapt to that environment's requirements – from different writing systems to the characteristics of whatever medium is used.
sentences.translation: 此外，它会适应那个环境的要求——从不同书写系统到所使用的任何媒介的特征。

structure.text: Further
it
will adapt to that environment's requirements
from different writing systems to the characteristics of whatever medium is used
whatever medium is used

difficult_points: it 指代上一句的 reading circuit。
adapt to 表示阅读回路会随环境要求而调整。
破折号后的 from...to... 列出环境要求的范围。
whatever medium is used 是名词性从句，表示无论使用何种媒介。

sentences.sentence: If the dominant medium advantages processes that are fast, multi-task oriented and well-suited for large volumes of information, like the current digital medium, so will the reading circuit.
sentences.translation: 如果主导媒介有利于快速、以多任务为导向并适合处理大量信息的过程，就像当前数字媒介那样，那么阅读回路也会如此。

structure.text: If the dominant medium advantages processes
that are fast, multi-task oriented and well-suited for large volumes of information
like the current digital medium
so will the reading circuit

difficult_points: advantages 在这里作动词，表示“使……占优势”。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 processes，列出这些过程的特点。
so will the reading circuit 是倒装结构，意为阅读回路也会偏向这些过程。
like the current digital medium 是例子，说明 dominant medium 的典型形式。

sentences.sentence: As UCLA psychologist Patricia Greenfield writes, the result is that less attention and time will be allocated to slower, time-demanding deep reading processes.
sentences.translation: 正如加州大学洛杉矶分校心理学家帕特里夏·格林菲尔德所写，其结果是，更少的注意力和时间会被分配给较慢、耗时的深度阅读过程。

structure.text: As UCLA psychologist Patricia Greenfield writes
the result
is that less attention and time will be allocated to slower, time-demanding deep reading processes
that less attention and time will be allocated to slower, time-demanding deep reading processes

difficult_points: As 引出引用来源，说明观点来自 Patricia Greenfield。
is that... 是表语从句结构，说明 result 的具体内容。
will be allocated 是被动结构，强调注意力和时间被分配给何处。
slower, time-demanding 修饰 deep reading processes，突出其与数字媒介偏好的冲突。

sentences.sentence: Increasing reports from educators and from researchers in psychology and the humanities bear this out.
sentences.translation: 来自教育工作者以及心理学和人文学科研究人员的越来越多报告证实了这一点。

structure.text: Increasing reports from educators and from researchers in psychology and the humanities
bear this out

difficult_points: Increasing reports 是长主语，表示证据数量正在增加。
from educators and from researchers... 并列说明报告来源。
bear this out 是固定表达，this 指代上一段关于深度阅读受影响的观点。

sentences.sentence: English literature scholar and teacher Mark Edmundson describes how many college students actively avoid the classic literature of the 19th and 20th centuries in favour of something simpler as they no longer have the patience to read longer, denser, more difficult texts.
sentences.translation: 英国文学学者兼教师马克·埃德蒙森描述了许多大学生如何主动避开 19 世纪和 20 世纪的经典文学，转而选择更简单的东西，因为他们不再有耐心阅读更长、更密集、更困难的文本。

structure.text: English literature scholar and teacher Mark Edmundson
describes
how many college students actively avoid the classic literature of the 19th and 20th centuries in favour of something simpler
as they no longer have the patience to read longer, denser, more difficult texts

difficult_points: English literature scholar and teacher 是 Mark Edmundson 的身份说明，与人名共同构成主语。
how 引导 describes 的宾语从句，说明他描述的现象。
in favour of something simpler 表示放弃经典文学而偏向更简单内容。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释学生回避难文本的原因。

sentences.sentence: We should be less concerned with students' 'cognitive impatience', however, than by what may underlie it: the potential inability of large numbers of students to read with a level of critical analysis sufficient to comprehend the complexity of thought and argument found in more demanding texts.
sentences.translation: 然而，相比学生的“认知急躁”，我们更应担心其背后可能存在的东西：大量学生可能没有能力以足够的批判性分析水平阅读，从而理解更高要求文本中思想和论证的复杂性。

structure.text: We
should be less concerned with students' 'cognitive impatience'
however
than by what may underlie it
the potential inability of large numbers of students to read with a level of critical analysis sufficient to comprehend the complexity of thought and argument found in more demanding texts
found in more demanding texts

difficult_points: less concerned with... than by... 表示真正更值得担心的是后者。
what may underlie it 是名词性从句，it 指 cognitive impatience。
冒号后解释“背后的东西”具体是什么。
sufficient to comprehend... 修饰 a level of critical analysis，说明分析水平需要达到的程度。

sentences.sentence: Multiple studies show that digital screen use may be causing a variety of troubling downstream effects on reading comprehension in older high school and college students.
sentences.translation: 多项研究表明，数字屏幕使用可能正在对高中高年级学生和大学生的阅读理解产生多种令人担忧的下游影响。

structure.text: Multiple studies
show
that digital screen use may be causing a variety of troubling downstream effects on reading comprehension in older high school and college students

difficult_points: that 引导 show 的宾语从句，说明研究发现。
may be causing 表示可能正在造成影响，保留研究结论的谨慎性。
downstream effects 指由屏幕使用进一步引发的后续影响。
in older high school and college students 限定研究关注的人群。

sentences.sentence: In Stavanger, Norway, psychologist Anne Mangen and her colleagues studied how high school students comprehend the same material in different mediums.
sentences.translation: 在挪威斯塔万格，心理学家安妮·曼根和她的同事研究了高中生如何在不同媒介中理解同一材料。

structure.text: In Stavanger, Norway
psychologist Anne Mangen and her colleagues
studied
how high school students comprehend the same material in different mediums

difficult_points: In Stavanger, Norway 提供研究地点。
psychologist Anne Mangen and her colleagues 是并列主语结构。
how 引导 studied 的宾语从句，说明研究问题。
the same material in different mediums 对比的是媒介差异，而不是文本内容差异。

sentences.sentence: Mangen's group asked subjects questions about a short story whose plot had universal student appeal; half of the students read the story on a tablet, the other half in paperback.
sentences.translation: 曼根的团队向受试者提问，问题关于一个情节对学生普遍有吸引力的短篇故事；一半学生在平板上读这个故事，另一半则读平装本。

structure.text: Mangen's group
asked subjects questions about a short story
whose plot had universal student appeal
half of the students read the story on a tablet
the other half in paperback

difficult_points: whose 引导定语从句，修饰 a short story，说明其情节吸引学生。
分号连接实验设计中的两个步骤或条件。
the other half in paperback 是省略句，完整意思是 the other half read the story in paperback。
subjects 指实验受试者，不是“科目”。

sentences.sentence: Results indicated that students who read on print were superior in their comprehension to screen-reading peers, particularly in their ability to sequence detail and reconstruct the plot in chronological order.
sentences.translation: 结果表明，阅读纸质文本的学生在理解方面优于读屏幕的同伴，尤其是在按顺序排列细节并按时间顺序重构情节的能力上。

structure.text: Results
indicated
that students who read on print were superior in their comprehension to screen-reading peers
who read on print
particularly in their ability to sequence detail and reconstruct the plot in chronological order

difficult_points: that 引导 indicated 的宾语从句，说明实验结果。
who read on print 修饰 students，区分纸质阅读组。
were superior...to... 表示纸质阅读组优于屏幕阅读组。
particularly in... 具体说明优势体现在哪些能力上。

sentences.sentence: Ziming Liu from San Jose State University has conducted a series of studies which indicate that the 'new norm' in reading is skimming, involving word-spotting and browsing through the text.
sentences.translation: 圣何塞州立大学的刘子明进行了一系列研究，这些研究表明，阅读中的“新常态”是略读，包括找词和浏览文本。

structure.text: Ziming Liu from San Jose State University
has conducted a series of studies
which indicate that the 'new norm' in reading is skimming
that the 'new norm' in reading is skimming
involving word-spotting and browsing through the text

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，修饰 studies，说明这些研究的发现。
that 引导 indicate 的宾语从句，说明“新常态”的内容。
involving... 是现在分词短语，解释 skimming 包含的具体行为。
word-spotting 指只抓关键词而非连续深入阅读。

sentences.sentence: Many readers now use a pattern when reading in which they sample the first line and then word-spot through the rest of the text.
sentences.translation: 如今许多读者在阅读时采用一种模式：他们先抽样看第一行，然后在文本其余部分中寻找关键词。

structure.text: Many readers
now use a pattern
when reading
in which they sample the first line and then word-spot through the rest of the text

difficult_points: when reading 是省略结构，完整意思是 when they are reading。
in which 引导定语从句，修饰 a pattern，解释这种阅读模式。
sample the first line 表示只取样式地看第一行。
word-spot through 表示在文本中寻找词而非完整阅读。

sentences.sentence: When the reading brain skims like this, it reduces time allocated to deep reading processes.
sentences.translation: 当阅读大脑像这样略读时，它会减少分配给深度阅读过程的时间。

structure.text: When the reading brain skims like this
it
reduces time allocated to deep reading processes
allocated to deep reading processes

difficult_points: When 从句说明条件或时间背景。
it 指代 the reading brain。
allocated to... 是过去分词短语，修饰 time。
deep reading processes 与前文 skimming 形成对比。

sentences.sentence: In other words, we don't have time to grasp complexity, to understand another's feelings, to perceive beauty, and to create thoughts of the reader's own.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，我们没有时间把握复杂性、理解他人的感受、感知美，也没有时间形成读者自己的思想。

structure.text: In other words
we
don't have time
to grasp complexity, to understand another's feelings, to perceive beauty, and to create thoughts of the reader's own

difficult_points: In other words 表示用更直接的话解释上一句的后果。
don't have time 后接四个并列不定式，列出被牺牲的深度阅读能力。
another's feelings 表示他人的感受。
the reader's own 强调思想应由读者自己生成。

sentences.sentence: The possibility that critical analysis, empathy and other deep reading processes could become the unintended 'collateral damage' of our digital culture is not a straightforward binary issue about print versus digital reading.
sentences.translation: 批判性分析、共情以及其他深度阅读过程可能成为数字文化无意造成的“附带损害”，这种可能性并不是一个关于纸质阅读与数字阅读的简单二元问题。

structure.text: The possibility
that critical analysis, empathy and other deep reading processes could become the unintended 'collateral damage' of our digital culture
is not a straightforward binary issue about print versus digital reading

difficult_points: that 从句是 The possibility 的同位语，解释这种可能性是什么。
could become 表示潜在后果，而不是已经确定发生。
collateral damage 原指附带损害，这里比喻数字文化无意中伤害深度阅读。
not a straightforward binary issue 再次否定简单的纸质与数字二分法。

sentences.sentence: It is about how we all have begun to read on various mediums and how that changes not only what we read, but also the purposes for which we read.
sentences.translation: 它关乎我们所有人如何开始在各种媒介上阅读，以及这如何不仅改变我们读什么，也改变我们为了什么而读。

structure.text: It
is about how we all have begun to read on various mediums
and how that changes not only what we read, but also the purposes for which we read
not only what we read, but also the purposes for which we read

difficult_points: It 指代前一句所说的问题本质。
两个 how 从句并列，说明问题关乎阅读方式及其后果。
not only...but also... 强调改变的不只是阅读内容，还有阅读目的。
for which we read 是定语从句，修饰 purposes。

sentences.sentence: Nor is it only about the young.
sentences.translation: 它也并不仅仅关乎年轻人。

structure.text: Nor
is it
only about the young

difficult_points: Nor 置于句首触发倒装，所以使用 is it 而不是 it is。
it 指代数字阅读影响深度阅读的问题。
only about the young 否定影响只限于年轻人。

sentences.sentence: The subtle atrophy of critical analysis and empathy affects us all equally.
sentences.translation: 批判性分析和共情能力的微妙萎缩同样影响着我们所有人。

structure.text: The subtle atrophy of critical analysis and empathy
affects us all equally

difficult_points: atrophy 本指萎缩，这里比喻认知和情感能力的退化。
of critical analysis and empathy 修饰 atrophy，说明萎缩发生在哪些能力上。
us all equally 强调这种影响不限年龄或群体。

sentences.sentence: It affects our ability to navigate a constant bombardment of information.
sentences.translation: 它影响我们应对持续信息轰炸的能力。

structure.text: It
affects our ability
to navigate a constant bombardment of information

difficult_points: It 指代上一句的 subtle atrophy。
to navigate... 修饰 ability，说明这种能力的内容。
bombardment of information 比喻信息大量、持续地袭来。

sentences.sentence: It incentivizes a retreat to the most familiar stores of unchecked information, which require and receive no analysis, leaving us susceptible to false information and irrational ideas.
sentences.translation: 它促使人们退回到最熟悉的、未经核查的信息库中，而这些信息既不需要也得不到分析，从而使我们容易受到虚假信息和非理性观念的影响。

structure.text: It
incentivizes a retreat to the most familiar stores of unchecked information
which require and receive no analysis
leaving us susceptible to false information and irrational ideas

difficult_points: It 仍指 critical analysis and empathy 的 atrophy。
incentivizes a retreat to... 表示这种萎缩会鼓励人们退回熟悉的信息来源。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 stores of unchecked information。
leaving us susceptible... 是现在分词短语，表示前述行为造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: There's an old rule in neuroscience that does not alter with age: use it or lose it.
sentences.translation: 神经科学中有一条不会随年龄改变的老规则：用进废退。

structure.text: There's an old rule in neuroscience
that does not alter with age
use it or lose it

difficult_points: There's 是 There is 的缩写，构成存在句。
that does not alter with age 修饰 an old rule，说明规则不随年龄改变。
冒号后的 use it or lose it 是对 old rule 的具体说明。

sentences.sentence: It is a very hopeful principle when applied to critical thought in the reading brain because it implies choice.
sentences.translation: 当这一原则应用于阅读大脑中的批判性思维时，它是一个非常令人充满希望的原则，因为它意味着选择。

structure.text: It
is a very hopeful principle
when applied to critical thought in the reading brain
because it implies choice

difficult_points: It 指代上一句的 use it or lose it 原则。
when applied... 是省略结构，完整含义是 when it is applied。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么这一原则令人有希望。
choice 指人们可以通过使用和训练来保留阅读能力。

sentences.sentence: The story of the changing reading brain is hardly finished.
sentences.translation: 阅读大脑变化的故事远未结束。

structure.text: The story of the changing reading brain
is hardly finished

difficult_points: The story 是比喻表达，指阅读大脑变化的发展过程。
hardly finished 表示远未结束，暗示未来仍可干预。
changing reading brain 中 changing 修饰 reading brain，强调其可塑性。

sentences.sentence: We possess both the science and the technology to identify and redress the changes in how we read before they become entrenched.
sentences.translation: 我们既拥有科学也拥有技术，可以在这些阅读方式变化变得根深蒂固之前识别并纠正它们。

structure.text: We
possess both the science and the technology
to identify and redress the changes in how we read
how we read
before they become entrenched

difficult_points: both...and... 强调科学和技术两方面资源都具备。
to identify and redress... 表示这些资源的用途。
how we read 是名词性从句，作 changes in 的内容。
before they become entrenched 表示要在变化固化之前采取行动。

sentences.sentence: If we work to understand exactly what we will lose, alongside the extraordinary new capacities that the digital world has brought us, there is as much reason for excitement as caution.
sentences.translation: 如果我们努力准确理解自己会失去什么，同时也认识数字世界带给我们的非凡新能力，那么我们既有理由兴奋，也有理由谨慎。

structure.text: If we work to understand exactly what we will lose
what we will lose
alongside the extraordinary new capacities
that the digital world has brought us
there is as much reason for excitement as caution

difficult_points: If 从句提出条件：既要理解损失，也要看到数字世界带来的新能力。
what we will lose 是 understand 的宾语从句。
that the digital world has brought us 修饰 capacities，说明这些能力的来源。
as much reason for excitement as caution 表示兴奋和谨慎的理由同样充分。
